name: Intel MPX — Memory Protection Extensions (deprecated)
summary: Cautionary tale: Intel's hardware bounds-checking extension, deprecated and removed post-Tiger Lake. Failed for concrete reasons — up to 50% slowdown, only 4 bounds registers, no temporal safety, ABI-breaking, false positives, broken with multithreading. The MPX failure pattern directly informed MTE / CET / CHERI designs.

sections.title: Overview
Lessons for successor designs
sections.content:
The MPX failure pattern — too few hardware resources, no temporal coverage, ABI breakage — directly informed MTE / CET / CHERI designs:

- **MTE** uses per-allocation tags, not per-pointer bounds registers, sidestepping the register-pressure problem.
- **CET** is opt-in via `ENDBR` and shadow-stack pages, avoiding ABI breakage.
- **CHERI** changes the pointer representation itself rather than adding parallel bounds registers — bounds always travel with the pointer, no spill/fill.

MPX is the design point that every later hardware memory-safety architecture is implicitly defined against.
